items.title: The Suns lock in the last of their own priority free agents, finishing negotiations with Williams' Excel Sports Management agents Jeff Schwartz and Jordan Gertler on Thursday night. First young guards Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin over the weekend, now Williams.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: The Suns lock in the last of their own priority free agents, finishing negotiations with Williams' Excel Sports Management agents Jeff Schwartz and Jordan Gertler on Thursday night. First young guards Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin over the weekend, now Williams.

items.title: With estimates plugged in for Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin, I've got the Suns projected at $1.2 million over the luxury tax with two open standard roster spots. Phoenix projects to be roughly $6.7M under the first apron.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
With estimates plugged in for Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin, I've got the Suns projected at $1.2 million over the luxury tax with two open standard roster spots. Phoenix projects to be roughly $6.7M under the first apron.

Mark Williams is the team's main unsigned FA.

items.author: Sports Illustrated

items.title: By my projections Suns will be roughly $4.6M under the tax and $12.6M under the first apron after re-signing Collin Gillespie. Can create more room if they move on from Haywood Highsmith and/or Jamaree Bouyea.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
By my projections Suns will be roughly $4.6M under the tax and $12.6M under the first apron after re-signing Collin Gillespie. Can create more room if they move on from Haywood Highsmith and/or Jamaree Bouyea.
It'll get tight to avoid the first apron if they re-sign Mark Williams and Jordan Goodwin.

items.title: Sources: Collin Gillespie back to Suns for 4 years, $48M - via @ESPN App https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/49129656/sources-gillespie-returning-suns-4-year-48-million-deal
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: Sources: Collin Gillespie back to Suns for 4 years, $48M - via @ESPN App https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/49129656/sources-gillespie-returning-suns-4-year-48-million-deal

media.title: Sources: Gillespie returning to Suns on 4-year, $48 million deal
media.description: Collin Gillespie, who is coming off a breakout campaign with the Suns, intends to sign a four-year, $48 million deal to remain with the team, sources told ESPN on Saturday.

items.title: That's a good value for both Collin Gillespie and Phoenix. He earned a much bigger deal than the two-ways and minimums he's been on. Suns keep him for $12M AAV. Really solid.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
That's a good value for both Collin Gillespie and Phoenix. He earned a much bigger deal than the two-ways and minimums he's been on. Suns keep him for $12M AAV. Really solid.

If using standard raises, contract will look roughly like this:

26-27: $10.7M
27-28: $11.6 M
28-29: $12.4M
29-30: $13.3M

items.title: Collin Gillespie returns to Suns on 4-year, $48 million deal
items.author: Yahoo Sports
items.body_excerpt: Collin Gillespie returns to Suns on 4-year, $48 million deal Yahoo Sports

items.title: Most teams could've offered Collin Gillespie the $15.1 million non-taxpayer mid-level exception. The Suns could've topped such offers by having his Early Bird rights.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Most teams could've offered Collin Gillespie the $15.1 million non-taxpayer mid-level exception. The Suns could've topped such offers by having his Early Bird rights.

His deal comes in right around where he'd been projected at. T.J. McConnell's deal but adjust for cap inflation.

items.title: Phoenix Suns salary cap situation after re-signing Collin Gillespie.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Phoenix Suns salary cap situation after re-signing Collin Gillespie.

They are projected to be just $4 million under the $200.5 million luxury tax line.

They'll need to reduce payroll to re-sign Jordan Goodwin and fill up the roster while staying under the tax line.

items.title: [Charania] Free agent guard Collin Gillespie intends to sign a four-year, $48 million deal to return to the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. Gillespie spent three straight seasons on two-way contracts after going undrafted out of Villanova in 2022 and now earns a lucrative long-term deal.
items.author: MembershipSingle7137
